acknowledgementOfCountry: JATL acknowledge the Traditional Owners of the lands on which Alt+Law was created. We pay our respects to the Turrbal and Yuggera peoples, on whose land we live, work and study. We honour their Elders past and present, and we recognise the strength, resilience, and ongoing contributions of First Nations peoples to law, justice, education, and community life. We also acknowledge that sovereignty was never ceded. As students and emerging professionals, we commit to learning from First Nations knowledge systems, respecting cultural protocols, and contributing to a future grounded in justice, equity, and truth-telling.
welcomeIntro:
Welcome to Alt-Law: JATL's Student Careers Guide. This guide showcases the breadth of legal careers that exist beyond the traditionally promoted pathways of commercial law clerkships. Designed by law students, for law students, Alt-Law expands on the typical careers guides produced by student law societies by highlighting the full spectrum of legal practice and connecting students with the resources they need to shape a career aligned with their values and aspirations.

The Alt-Law project reflects JATL's commitment to innovation while remaining grounded in our core mission: promoting and critically examining the intersection between law and social justice. If you find this guide valuable and want to support its growth, we invite you to join JATL as a member so we can continue improving and expanding this resource.

JATL extends our sincere thanks to our sponsor, the College of Law. Their support has made the development of this careers guide - and our accompanying careers networking event - significantly easier and more impactful. You can explore additional College of Law resources in the External Resources section.

This work is an edited and expanded compilation of information based on a large number of sources. Alt+Law draws heavily on a variety of sources, including (but not limited to) information provided by the following sources, all of whom are attributed to the greatest extent possible throughout this Guide:

- The websites of various firms and organisations;
- Previous employees, employers, graduates and students;
- Equivalent versions of equivalent and related guides by Law Student Societies/Associations; and
- Information received from employers and organisations.

Any infringement on the intellectual property of any third party is only in good faith and purely accidental. Amendments can and will be made to remove any such intellectual property infringement following contact with JATL.

Alt+Law is prepared and distributed free of charge in the understanding that the Editor, authors and other individuals involved in the publication are in no way responsible for the results of their actions or omissions on the basis of any of the information provided. Continued reference to or use of this Guide indicates your assent to this understanding, and your unconditional release of any claims which you might wish to bring against the Editor or JATL in relation to this Guide or your use or reliance thereupon.

The reader of this Guide must be aware that opportunities and recruitment dates listed on Alt-Law may be subject to change.
